rules.name: Hardcoded API key in source
rules.message: An API key appears to be hardcoded in source. Move it to an environment variable.

rules.name: Hardcoded database URL with credentials
rules.message: Database URL with credentials found in source. Use environment variables.

rules.name: .env file committed to Git
rules.message: .env file is tracked by Git. Remove it and add to .gitignore.

rules.name: .env.local or .env.production committed
rules.message: Production env file is in Git history. Rotate all exposed secrets.

rules.name: .env missing from .gitignore
rules.message: .env files are not in .gitignore. Add them before you accidentally commit secrets.

rules.name: Private key committed
rules.message: A private key was found in source. Remove it, rotate it immediately, and add to .gitignore.

rules.name: Secret in Dockerfile ENV
rules.message: Secret hardcoded in Dockerfile ENV. Use build args or a secrets manager.

rules.name: Secret hardcoded in GitHub Actions YAML
rules.message: Secret hardcoded in CI workflow. Use ${{ secrets.YOUR_SECRET }} instead.

rules.name: Weak JWT secret
rules.message: JWT secret is too short or is a known weak value. Use a random 256-bit secret from env.

rules.name: Firebase API key exposed in client-side code
rules.message: Firebase config is client-side — ensure Firebase Security Rules are locked down.

rules.name: AWS access key in source
rules.message: AWS access key found. Revoke it immediately in IAM and use environment variables.

rules.name: Stripe live secret key in source
rules.message: Stripe live secret key found in code. Revoke it in your Stripe dashboard now.

rules.name: Secret in test files
rules.message: Credentials found in test files. Use mocks or environment variables even in tests.

rules.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_ env var contains secret
rules.message: NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables are exposed to the browser. Move secrets to server-only env vars.

rules.name: Secret committed in git history
rules.message: A secret was found in git history (even if deleted). Rotate the credential and rewrite history.

rules.name: No auth middleware on any API route
rules.message: No authentication middleware detected on any API route. Every non-public route needs auth.

rules.name: Admin route without role check
rules.message: Admin route found without a role-check middleware. Add RBAC before the handler.

rules.name: jwt.verify without error handling
rules.message: jwt.verify() is called without handling errors — invalid tokens will silently pass through.

rules.name: JWT stored in localStorage
rules.message: JWT in localStorage is readable by any JavaScript — XSS can steal it. Use httpOnly cookies.

rules.name: JWT with no expiry
rules.message: JWT has no expiry (expiresIn not set). Tokens will be valid forever if stolen.

rules.name: Cookie missing httpOnly flag
rules.message: Auth cookie has httpOnly: false — JavaScript can read it. Set httpOnly: true.

rules.name: Cookie missing secure flag
rules.message: Cookie can be sent over HTTP. Set secure: true to restrict to HTTPS only.

rules.name: Cookie missing sameSite
rules.message: Cookie missing sameSite attribute. Set 'strict' or 'lax' to reduce CSRF risk.

rules.name: Password stored without hashing
rules.message: User save detected without bcrypt/argon2. Passwords must be hashed before storage.

rules.name: MD5 or SHA1 used for passwords
rules.message: MD5/SHA1 are broken for password hashing. Use bcrypt or argon2.

rules.name: Weak session secret
rules.message: Express session using a known default secret. Set a long random secret from env.

rules.name: express-session using default MemoryStore
rules.message: MemoryStore leaks memory in production and is lost on restart. Use Redis or another store.

rules.name: console.log of password or token
rules.message: Credential logged to console. Remove immediately — this appears in logs and crash reporters.

rules.name: SQL string concatenation
rules.message: SQL built from string concatenation. Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

rules.name: MongoDB find with raw req.body
rules.message: Passing raw request data to MongoDB find() allows NoSQL injection. Validate and sanitize first.

rules.name: eval() with user input
rules.message: eval() with user input = remote code execution. Never use eval() with external data.

rules.name: child_process exec with string concat
rules.message: Shell command built from user input. Use execFile() with an args array instead.

rules.name: innerHTML assignment
rules.message: innerHTML can execute injected scripts. Use textContent or a sanitizer like DOMPurify.

rules.name: dangerouslySetInnerHTML in React
rules.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses React's XSS protection. Sanitize with DOMPurify first.

rules.name: Path traversal via fs.readFile
rules.message: File path from user input can be traversed (../../etc/passwd). Sanitize and use path.resolve with a basedir check.

rules.name: SSTI via raw template render
rules.message: User input passed directly to template engine = server-side template injection.

rules.name: JSON.parse on user input without try/catch
rules.message: JSON.parse() on user input can throw and crash your server. Wrap in try/catch.

rules.name: POST/PUT route body used without validation
rules.message: req.body used directly without a validation library (zod, joi, yup). Validate all input shapes.

rules.name: File upload without type restriction
rules.message: File upload accepts any file type. Add a fileFilter to allowlist safe MIME types.

rules.name: File upload without size limit
rules.message: No file size limit on upload. Set limits.fileSize in multer config to prevent DoS.

rules.name: Uploaded file saved with original filename
rules.message: Using originalname in the save path can lead to path traversal. Use a UUID instead.

rules.name: req.query used in DB query without validation
rules.message: Query params passed to DB without validation. Cast types and validate before querying.

rules.name: helmet not used in Express
rules.message: Helmet is not configured. It sets 11 security headers automatically. Add app.use(helmet()).

rules.name: CORS wildcard origin
rules.message: CORS is open to all origins. Restrict to your specific domains in production.

rules.name: CORS wildcard with credentials:true
rules.message: CORS with origin:'*' and credentials:true is invalid and dangerous. Specify the exact origin.

rules.name: HTTP used for external calls
rules.message: External request over plain HTTP — data is sent unencrypted. Use HTTPS.

rules.name: TLS verification disabled
rules.message: TLS verification is disabled — MITM attacks are possible. Remove this line entirely.

rules.name: No CSP header configured
rules.message: No Content-Security-Policy found. CSP is your last line of defense against XSS.

rules.name: X-Powered-By header not disabled
rules.message: Express advertises itself via X-Powered-By. Disable with app.disable('x-powered-by').

rules.name: No rate limiting on any public route
rules.message: No rate limiting detected. Any endpoint can be hammered indefinitely.

rules.name: No rate limiting on auth routes
rules.message: Auth routes (login, register, forgot-password) have no rate limit. Brute force is trivially possible.

rules.name: No request body size limit
rules.message: express.json() has no size limit. A large payload can exhaust server memory.

rules.name: No timeout on external HTTP calls
rules.message: External API call with no timeout can hang your server indefinitely.

rules.name: Dependency with known Critical CVE
rules.message: A dependency has a Critical CVE. Run npm audit fix or upgrade the package.

rules.name: Dependency with known High CVE
rules.message: A dependency has a High severity vulnerability. Review and patch.

rules.name: Package version pinned to wildcard
rules.message: Wildcard version (*) in package.json allows any version including breaking or malicious ones.

rules.name: No package-lock.json committed
rules.message: No lockfile found. Without it, installs are non-deterministic and supply-chain attacks are easier.

rules.name: postinstall script fetches from internet
rules.message: postinstall script downloads from the internet — supply chain risk. Review carefully.

rules.name: Stack trace returned in error response
rules.message: Error stack trace returned to client exposes internal paths and code structure.

rules.name: verbose error messages (user enumeration)
rules.message: User-specific error messages allow account enumeration. Use generic 'Invalid credentials'.

rules.name: .git exposed via static file server
rules.message: .git directory served statically — attackers can download your entire source code.

rules.name: Dockerfile runs as root
rules.message: Container runs as root. Add a USER directive to drop to a non-root user.

rules.name: .env not in .dockerignore
rules.message: .env is not in .dockerignore — it may be baked into your Docker image.

rules.name: debug mode active in code
rules.message: Debug mode appears to be enabled. Ensure this is off in production.

rules.name: New DB client per request (no pooling)
rules.message: Creating a new DB connection per request exhausts connection limits. Use a persistent pool.

rules.name: Dynamic column name in SELECT
rules.message: Column name built from user input = column injection. Use a whitelist of allowed column names.

rules.name: User-controlled orderBy passed to ORM
rules.message: User-controlled sort field. Whitelist allowed sort columns to prevent injection or info disclosure.

rules.name: Math.random() used for security token
rules.message: Math.random() is predictable. Use crypto.randomBytes() for security-sensitive tokens.

rules.name: MD5 used in security context
rules.message: MD5 is cryptographically broken. Use SHA-256 or better for integrity, bcrypt for passwords.

rules.name: AES-ECB mode used
rules.message: AES-ECB mode reveals patterns in ciphertext. Use AES-GCM or AES-CBC with a random IV.

rules.name: bcrypt rounds too low
rules.message: bcrypt with fewer than 10 rounds is too fast for password hashing. Use at least 10-12.

rules.name: Hardcoded IV in cipher
rules.message: Hardcoded IV makes encryption deterministic. Generate a random IV per encryption operation.

rules.name: No CSRF protection on state-changing routes
rules.message: No CSRF middleware found on form submission routes. Use csurf or double-submit cookies.

rules.name: Logging entire req.body (includes passwords)
rules.message: Logging req.body exposes passwords and sensitive data. Redact sensitive fields before logging.

rules.name: Logging entire req.headers (includes auth tokens)
rules.message: Logging headers exposes Authorization tokens. Redact the Authorization header.

rules.name: No error tracking configured
rules.message: No error tracking SDK (Sentry, Datadog, etc.) detected. Errors in production will be invisible.

rules.name: No .gitignore file
rules.message: No .gitignore found. You will accidentally commit secrets, node_modules, and build artifacts.

rules.name: node_modules committed to Git
rules.message: node_modules is tracked by Git. Add it to .gitignore and run git rm -r --cached node_modules.

rules.name: Stripe webhook missing signature verification
rules.message: Stripe webhook handler lacks constructEvent() verification. Anyone can fake a payment event.

rules.name: Stripe test key used in production config
rules.message: Stripe test key found in production config file. Payments will silently fail.

rules.name: Raw card data passed through server
rules.message: Raw card data must never touch your server. Use Stripe.js / Elements to tokenize on the client.
